---
# The title MUST start with "FIMS Weekly - "
title: "FIMS Weekly - March 09–13, 2026"
date: "March 09, 2026"
# Change the following categories accordingly, but you MUST keep fims-weekly
categories:
  - fims-weekly
  - manuscripts
three-big-things:
  bt1: "All changes to the code that are going to happen for the CIE Review must happen this week. Time-varying selectivity and time-varying weight-at-age inputs might make it in :crossed_fingers:. Next week will be focused on updating the case studies."
  bt2: "Nathan and Andrea are working on a PR to increase the usability of the random effects in FIMS."
  bt3: "We have addressed the comments we received on the FIMS manuscript from reviewers and it is time to do a final round of edits. The [manuscript](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1HyLJa4y8fkKML31a636qwozkaYXglT9yzQCmE6bTLa0/edit?usp=drive_link) and the responses to [reviewers](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1t_eju7vqF119ZPgLVU95pQFqjzGRTIMmQo0lucTqEbQ/edit?usp=drive_link) are on Google Drive. Please provide all comments by March 18, 2026 so we can return the manuscript to ICES by March 19, 2026."
# Announcements go below because of formatting that quarto doesnt transfer over from the yml
# If there is no code club or seaside chat, just delete those lines and the template will handle it for you
upcoming-events:
# If you have any additional events, uncomment and fill out the additional events section below.
# If there is no actual photo you can delete include-photo, path, and alt-text 
# and the template will handle it for you. 
# If there is code instead, please uncomment the section below named photo-code 
# but still include the text portion under photo.
photo:
  text: "Bai recently experimented with using quarto to write a manuscript and found the [template for Elsevier Journals](https://quarto-journals.github.io/elsevier/) easy to use. All the code is on [GitHub](https://github.com/quarto-journals/elsevier) and you can implement the template in your own repo with the following, single, line of code within a terminal. Quarto also provides [templates for other journals](https://quarto.org/docs/journals/) and information regarding how to use templates on their website. Thank you Bai for sharing."
---
  
{{< include "fims-weekly-template.qmd" >}}

::: {#announcements}
- 43 days until the CIE Review.
- We will be paring the rendered case studies down to just three, or four, examples for the CIE Review to ensure that they are fully developed and provide well-explained examples of comparisons of FIMS models to existing platforms. The remainder of the examples will stay in the repository but will not be showcased on the resulting website. This work will happen next week.
::: 
    
::: {#additional-events}
### Wednesday, March 12
**FIMS Code Club**

Time: 11:00–14:00 E; 08:00–11:00 P; 09:00–12:00 AK; 05:00-08:00 H
Location: Virtual
Online: [Google meet](https://meet.google.com/unsupported?meetingCode=ica-ieta-ndu&ref=https://meet.google.com/ica-ieta-ndu)
:::


::: {#photo-code}
```bash
quarto use template quarto-journals/elsevier
```
:::
